# Drift-sentinel spec input: self-description surfaces in scope + the four-strike labeled corpus from the first live specimen

## Context

The v14 drift-sentinel (mapped in the #13444 horizon: detection consumer + render semantics) now has what it was missing: a real, fully-forensic'd specimen. The near-erasure incident (#14736 / PR #14740; forensics + convergence on #13444) supplies both the scope correction and the acceptance corpus.

## Spec input

1. **Scope includes self-description SURFACES, not just graph state:** blog prose, memory files, PR/ticket biography — the incident's fabrications lived entirely in prose and file layers while the graph trail stayed clean. A sentinel watching only graph state would have seen nothing.
2. **The labeled corpus (four sub-classes, one incident):** fabricated origin (biography contradicting the bearer's own trail) · pole-swing over-correction (opposite-sign fabrication during repair) · peer-agency erasure (frame-import rewriting OTHERS' recorded choices) · uncited peer claim (surviving post-recovery supervision — the claim-unit proof). Fire/record semantics per the recorded discriminator: a recorded transition never fires; a trail-less contradiction fires.
3. **The bus-factor argument:** in the live incident the OPERATOR was the sentinel. That is bus factor 1 for identity integrity — the standing justification for building the mechanical one.
4. **Narrative gravity as a detection prior:** the fabrication matched the artifact's own thesis (proven at maximum difficulty — the thesis WARNED against the failure and induced it anyway). Self-description inside a live narrative frame deserves a raised prior.

## Acceptance Criteria (for the sentinel leaf when filed/claimed)

- [ ] All four corpus classes detected on the incident's actual text/record pairs (the fixture set).
- [ ] Recorded-transition fixtures do NOT fire (growth stays legal — the §4 boundary).
- [ ] Surface coverage includes prose + memory files, not graph-only.

## Related

#13444 (horizon map + convergence record) · #14677 (identity substrate) · #14736 / PR #14740 (the specimen) · the identity-claim citation discipline ticket (the prevention half; this is the detection half).

Claimable — v14 line; blocked on nothing (spec-input is complete), implementation sequencing per the epic owner's tranches.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-04T22:45:58Z

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT] Intake verdict: **valuable specimen/corpus, not code-ready as a branchable implementation ticket yet**. I applied `not-code-ready` + `needs-design` so this does not keep surfacing as a no-assignee implementation lane until the actual detection surface is pinned.

V-B-A evidence checked before this classification:
- Live #14753 body/comments: no comments, no assignee, no close PRs, no Contract Ledger, and the AC heading says "for the sentinel leaf when filed/claimed" rather than defining this issue as that leaf.
- #13444 is the v14 home/COP epic and contains the drift-specimen convergence comments; its native subissue graph does **not** include #14753 today.
- #14736 is closed by merged PR #14740, confirming the specimen exists and was corrected.
- #14752 is closed by merged PR #14756, confirming the prevention half already landed. Its contract explicitly names "the future #14753 lint" / "#14753's mechanical lint landing" as a later detection trigger and calibration corpus.
- Repo search finds the corpus already recorded in `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/identity-claim-audit.md` and the post-compaction clause in `context-recovery-workflow.md`; no existing open PR closes #14753.
- Duplicate/successor sweep found related authority surfaces (#13444, #14677, #14752/#14756, #14740) but no branchable detection/lint ticket with a concrete target surface.

Why this blocks branch work: the ticket is framed as **spec input** and evidence transfer. It does not yet define whether the implementation target is a `pr-review` lint, a Skill Manifest lint extension, a drift-sentinel service, a render-side probe, a test fixture suite, or some combination. Because the intended detector would be consumed by agents/reviewers and likely affects identity-governance substrate, the Contract Ledger gate applies before implementation.

Re-entry request: update the body or file/link a child implementation ticket with a Contract Ledger covering at least:

| Target Surface | Contract detail needed before implementation |
|---|---|
| Mechanical detector/lint | exact tool/script/service name, invocation surface, trigger condition, and fail/pass output shape |
| Fixture corpus | concrete fixture files or generated test inputs for the four classes; source anchors for each class |
| Surface coverage | which inputs are in scope for v1: PR body, issue body, blog/docs markdown, memory files, skill files, graph facts, render output |
| Non-fire cases | recorded-transition fixtures that must not flag, plus how the detector distinguishes growth from contradiction |
| Citation rule integration | whether this consumes `identity-claim-audit.md`, `pr-review-guide.md` §7.5.2, or a new lint rule; no duplicate rule vocabulary |
| Ownership/topology | whether #14753 becomes a child of #13444 or #14677, or whether a new child leaf owns implementation under those authorities |
| Evidence | exact unit/lint command and expected proof class; no reliance on manual review alone |

Current classification: `needs-narrowing` / `needs-contract-alignment` via available labels `not-code-ready` + `needs-design`. The corpus itself is useful; the issue just is not implementation-ready as written.
